claim.hypothesis: ...phasic auditory alertness affects visual perception by increasing the visual processing speed and lowering the threshold of conscious perception
claim.statement: ...an increase in processing speed v, t(27) = 4.71, p < 0.001, dz = 0.89, and a decrease in the perceptual threshold t0, t(27) = 3.52, p = 0.002, dz = 0.67, when a 85 dB cue is presented prior to the letter.

data.source: Self-generated data from running the experiment
data.access_details: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found, in the online version, at http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.cognition.2017.04.004.
data.notes: 23 females, 5 males, mean age = 22.8 years, SD = 1.8 years.

method.description: The authors conducted an experiment in which participants were to report the identity of a post-masked letter presented for varying exposure durations.
method.steps: (1) Recruit participants; (2) Set up the room for the experiment and have each participant stare at the fixed point on the monitor; (3) Run the main experiment where the participants determines the letter either with no sound (control) or with a beep (treated); (4) Run analysis on the data from the experiment using paired t-tests and maximum likelihood estimation
method.models: maximum-likelihood estimation using the Nelder-Mead simplex optimization algorithm; high-threshold guessing model; paired t-tests

results.summary: There was faster processing speed and lower perception threshold for the treated group

metadata.original_paper_title: The effect of phasic auditory alerting on visual perception.
